214.description: Predict NFL playoff contenders with odds to make the postseason at FanDuel Sportsbook. Back your favorite teams and celebrate their success!
214.footer:
Sportsbook Odds / NFL Odds / NFL Playoffs Odds

Odds to Make NFL Playoffs

Bet on which NFL teams are going to make it to the NFL Playoffs with FanDuel Sportsbook.

The early 2025–26 market has drawn a clear line between perceived locks and bubble teams. The Buffalo Bills top the board at –750 to reach the postseason (roughly an 88% implied probability), while the Baltimore Ravens (–480), Philadelphia Eagles (–420) and Kansas City Chiefs (–400) round out a tier that FanDuel Sportsbook views as near-certainties. Oddsmakers have faith in the Detroit Lions' updated coaching staff, which is reflected in the –200 odds, and San Francisco lurks just behind at –184 despite operating in the gauntlet that is the NFC West.

On the flipside, we have the odds to miss the playoffs for teams staring at extremely long playoff odds. The New York Giants leads the fade parade at –1800 to miss NFL Playoff football, followed closely by the Cleveland Browns (–1600) and New Orleans Saints (–1250). The New York Jets (–750) and Tennessee Titans (–590) round out a group that would need major turnarounds to cash a postseason ticket. Mid-tier fades such as Las Vegas (–420), Carolina (–300) and Indianapolis (–225) remind bettors that rebuilding rosters can still offer value, and one breakout quarterback or defensive leap can erase preseason pessimism in a hurry.

How NFL Playoff Odds Work - Reading the “Make or Miss” Market

FanDuel Sportsbook posts two-way markets on every NFL club: Odds to Make the Playoffs and Odds to Miss the Playoffs. These are moneyline prices, so a minus sign (-) shows how much you must stake to win $100, while a plus sign (+) shows the profit on a $100 wager. Playoff odds open moments after the draft and can move after a single marquee signing or major injury. Setting up alerts on NFL news lets you pounce when numbers drift.

As an example, if you bet $160 on the Tennessee Titans to make the playoffs at -160 odds, that bet would pay out $260 (your original $160 stake and $100 in winnings). If you wagered $100 on the Miami Dolphins to make the playoffs at +118 odds, you would earn $218 (your original $100 bet and $118 in winnings).


NFL Playoff Futures FAQs


  • When does my Make Playoff or Miss Playoff bet settle?

    FanDuel settles the market as soon as the league publishes its final postseason field using the NFL’s own tiebreakers. Subsequent league appeals or statistical revisions don’t change the payout.

  • Can I cash out my playoff futures early?

    Often, yes. When the Cash-Out button appears, you can lock in a profit or cut losses instantly. The request isn’t guaranteed (odds can move or the market can suspend) but if it’s accepted, the offer shown is final and your bet is settled on the spot.

  • Can I combine “Make the Playoffs” odds in a parlay?

    Sure, but remember parlay rules still apply. If one leg is void (say, a team becomes ineligible because the format changes), that leg drops out and the parlay is re-priced on the remaining selections. If multiple legs become correlated because of a schedule change, FanDuel may void or resettle the ticket to keep things fair.

Super Bowl 2027 Betting - Super Bowl Odds, Spreads, Moneylines, and Props

The NFL season is here and FanDuel Sportsbook has all the odds you need to bet on Super Bowl 61. Find the latest online sportsbook odds for the 2027 Super Bowl game here at FanDuel Sportsbook. As the season progresses and the matchup is determined, you can bet on the who will win the Super Bowl in 2026 and how each quarter will play out, whether that's through the spread or moneyline. You can also bet on Super Bowl prop bets such as player props, game props, and fun novelty props. At FanDuel Sportsbook, you have more ways to win when betting on the Super Bowl.

2027 Super Bowl Odds to Win

Fresh off their championship win, the Seattle Seahawks open as the +750 favorites to repeat in Super Bowl LXI. They are closely pursued by the Los Angeles Rams (+800), who will be fighting for the chance to play for the Lombardi Trophy on their home turf. Rounding out the top contenders are the Baltimore Ravens and Buffalo Bills, both sitting at +1200, followed by the Green Bay Packers at +1300 and the Los Angeles Chargers at +1500. Check back throughout the 2026-27 season to see how trades, injuries, and performance impact these championship futures.

Super Bowl Point Spreads and Lines

Once we know which two NFL teams will play in Super Bowl 61, FanDuel Sportsbook has officially released NFL game lines and point spreads for the big game. These odds are similar to the all NFL odds seen during the regular season and throughout the NFL Playoffs.

Any favorite’s point spread is displayed with a “minus” symbol next to a whole number or a halved fraction, such as (-6) or (-6.5). Bettors picking the favorite “against the spread” must hope that the team wins by more than 6 points – a 1 through 6-point victory or a straight-up loss loses the wager. An underdog’s point spread is displayed with a “+” symbol, indicating the number of points “spotted” to the team before the Super Bowl kicks off. Underdog point-spread gamblers are hoping that the NFL team will “cover” by winning by any amount or losing by any amount smaller than the betting line. If the underdog’s point spread is (+6) and the team loses by 5 points, the bettors still win the payout.

If a game finishes with the favorites winning by the exact line margin shown on the point spread, bets are returned in an outcome known as a “push.”


Reading Super Bowl Moneyline Odds

Moneyline odds promise a % payoff when a sports bettor's chosen team wins the game straight-up. A favorite’s moneyline NFL odds are denoted with a “-“ as in (-200). Underdog moneylines are marked with a “+” as in a ML of (+350). Payoff is calculated differently for the betting odds in the favorite’s and underdog’s markets, but always centered around the value of $100.

For instance, (-150) Super Bowl odds mean that for every $150 wagered on a team, the house will pay out $100 if that team wins. (-200) odds stand for $100 payoff on every $200 wagered at the betting book. On the underdog side, moneyline odds represent the amount paid out on won $100 bets if the team surprises. (+350) odds equal a $350 payout on $100, (+1000) odds represent $1000 payout on the same risk, and so on.


Super Bowl Betting FAQs


  • How to Bet on the Super Bowl?

    Super Bowl betting is simple and straightforward with FanDuel Sportsbook. Start by reviewing our Super Bowl betting tables above to find the latest odds and spreads for the big game. Once you've identified your top picks, add them to your betslip and submit your wager.

  • How are Super Bowl betting results determined?

    Super Bowl bets are based on the results and statistics provided by the league's governing body (www.nfl.com).

  • Where Will the Super Bowl be Played?

    Super Bowl 61 will be played at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California.

  • Who won Super Bowl 2026?

    The Seattle Seahawks defeated the New England Patriots, winning Super Bowl LX 29-13!

  • Where can I find all the rules for NFL betting?

    You can find all the rules for NFL betting in the House Rules section, located in the sportsbook menu.

NFL Parlays and Parlay Builder

Bet on NFL parlays at the home of the Same Game Parlay. Use the above betting markets to build your unique NFL parlay bet today. As you add more bets, check out the bet slip to see how your parlay odds adjust with each leg. Popular betting markets to parlay include:

  • Anytime Touchdown Scorer odds: Predict which player will find the endzone.
  • Alternative Spread odds: Adjust the point spread to your preferred risk level.
  • First Half/Quarter odds: Wager on the early game action for quick results.
  • Moneyline/Totals (Over/Under) odds: Combine outright winners or total points scored for broader strategies.

What Is An NFL Parlay Bet

An NFL parlay is a bet that combines two or more football betting selections into one larger bet. For a parlay to be successful, each of the individual legs (or selections) must win. With each added leg, your payout will increase, allowing you to get large payouts with lower wager amounts.

Today's NFL Odds – Bet on NFL Spreads, Moneylines, Totals and More

Looking for the latest NFL betting lines? FanDuel Sportsbook delivers accurate, real-time NFL odds for every game of the 2025-2026 season, including postseason matchups. Whether you're betting on full-game spreads, first-half results, or player props, our sports betting odds are created by industry-leading traders and adjusted live based on performance data, market movement, and betting trends.

As one of the official betting partners of the National Football League, FanDuel gives you direct access to Super Bowl odds, league futures, and MVP betting markets — plus thousands of markets on all 32 teams. With the Seattle Seahawks and New England Patriots set to clash in Super Bowl LX, there’s never been a better time to get in on the action.At FanDuel Sportsbook, you have more ways to win when you betting on the NFL.


How to Bet on NFL at FanDuel Sportsbook

At FanDuel, you can bet on NFL in ways that suit your strategy and level of experience. Here’s a clear beginner-to-intermediate guide on how to bet on the NFL, covering the core bet types and how to wager on the NFL at FanDuel Sportsbook.

Understanding the Main Types of NFL Bets

We offer a number of different betting markets for fans to pick from. Here are the most popular NFL bet types we offer:

  • NFL Spreads: The point spreads tries to level the playing field for both team's odds to win. A typical line might be: Chiefs -6.5 (+105) vs. Steelers +6.5 (-125). If you bet the Chiefs, they must win by 7+ points. If you back the Steelers, they can lose by 6 points—or win outright—and your bet cashes.

  • NFL Moneylines: These are the simplest form of NFL odds: pick the team you think will win outright. Favorites are denoted with a minus (-) and underdogs with a plus (+). For example: Lions -180 means you must wager $180 to win $100. Likewise, Bears +130 means a $100 bet returns $130 profit if they win.

  • NFL Totals (Over/Under): Bet on the total number of combined points in the game. If the Over/Under is set at 37.5: a 21-17 final score (38 combined points) hits the Over, while a 24-13 score (37 combined points) hits the Under.

  • Live NFL Odds (In-Game Betting): Watch and wager in real-time. Our in-play betting markets update between possessions and even between snaps. Bet on evolving outcomes like spreads, moneylines, totals, and player props as the game unfolds.

  • NFL Player Prop Bets: Target individual player stats such as passing yards for a QB, Anytime Touchdowns, rushing yards, total receptions, or interceptions. These markets are powered by official NFL data and constantly adjusted by FanDuel’s risk team to reflect real-world trends and injury status.

  • Parlays and Same Game Parlays: Combine multiple bets into a single wager for a bigger potential payout. Build your own Same Game Parlay for any NFL matchup by mixing player props, team totals, and game lines.

  • NFL Futures: Futures markets let you bet on long-term outcomes for the NFL regular season and playoffs. These include wagers such as the NFL Super Bowl winner, teams to win division titles, and which player will be crowned the NFL MVP.


Placing NFL Bets at FanDuel Sportsbook

With our website or mobile app, we make it easy to place bets on NFL events. Simply follow these steps:

  1. Sign up for a sportsbook account and veryify your ID and location if you haven't already

  2. Download the FanDuel Sportsbook app or login at sportsbook.fanduel.com on a desktop device

  3. Deposit funds

  4. Browse the NFL odds and props currently available. Click the odds to add them to your bet slip

  5. Enter a wager amount and add any promos or bonus bets you have available. The betslip will automatically calculate your potential payout. Click to place the bet when you are happy with your betslip

  6. Track your progress in the My Bets section


How to Read NFL Odds

NFL odds at FanDuel Sportsbook use the American odds format, shown as either positive (+) or negative (−) numbers:

  • +200: Wager $100 to win $200 (underdog).
  • -150: Wager $150 to win $100 (favorite).

Example: If you bet $100 on the Detroit Lions +200, you’d receive $300 total if they win: $200 in profit plus your $100 stake. Betting Philadelphia Eagles at −150 requires risking $150 to profit $100. FanDuel’s bet slip automatically calculates your payout, helping you make informed, fast decisions.


Why FanDuel is Trusted for NFL Betting


  • Trusted Market Leader: Official betting partner of the NFL.
  • Real-Time Odds: Adjusted by in-house analysts and informed by live market activity.
  • Flexible Markets: From player props to live Same Game Parlays and Super Bowl futures.
  • Responsible Gaming Tools: Deposit limits, timeouts, and support always available.

NFL Odds and Betting FAQs


  • Do overtime periods count for my NFL spread, moneyline, and totals wagers?

    Yes. FanDuel grades almost every NFL game market—including full-game spreads, moneylines, and over/unders—on the final official score after all overtime periods are completed, unless the market header specifically says otherwise.

  • What happens if an NFL game ends in a tie?

    If the final score is tied after overtime, moneyline wagers are void and stakes are refunded, while point-spread and totals bets are settled on the posted numbers (so a spread that lands exactly on the closing line is graded as a push).

  • What happens if an NFL game is postponed or moved to a different stadium?

    When a game is postponed before kickoff, all wagers are void. If it’s relocated but still played on the scheduled date—whether to a neutral site or the opponent’s stadium—bets remain live because FanDuel grades action on the official league box score, not the venue.

  • How does FanDuel treat a Same Game Parlay if one NFL leg is void (for example, because the game ended in a tie)?

    If a leg is voided, the parlay is re-priced to the odds of the remaining legs and still stands. Only when every leg in the SGP is void does FanDuel return the entire stake.

1990.title: Super Bowl Novelty Odds: Prop Bets, Coin Toss and More | FanDuel
1990.footer:
Sportsbook Odds / NFL Odds / NFL Novelty Betting Odds

NFL Novelty Betting Odds: Super Bowl Prop Bets and Fun Wagers

Dive into the exciting world of NFL Novelty Betting Odds at FanDuel Sportsbook, where the biggest moments get even more entertaining! Beyond traditional game lines and player props, novelty bets offer a unique way to engage with the NFL, especially during marquee events like the Super Bowl. These wagers focus on cultural and unusual outcomes that add a layer of fun and speculation to the game day experience. From the flip of a coin to the color of a celebratory drink, FanDuel brings you real-time odds on the quirky moments that become cultural touchstones.


What NFL Novelty Bets Are Available at FanDuel Sportsbook?

FanDuel Sportsbook offers a variety of engaging novelty bets, primarily centered around the Super Bowl. These unique prop bets allow you to wager on outcomes that don't directly impact the game's final score or player statistics, but certainly add to the spectacle. Currently, popular options often include:

  • Coin Toss Result: A classic 50/50 proposition, where you can bet on whether the coin will land on "Heads" or "Tails." While historically Tails has occurred slightly more often, each toss is an independent event!
  • Color of First Gatorade Poured on Winning Head Coach: A beloved Super Bowl tradition, this prop bet challenges you to predict the color of the Gatorade shower. Odds vary based on historical trends, team colors, and pure speculation, adding a vibrant element to your betting slip.

Keep an eye on the FanDuel platform as the Super Bowl approaches for these and other intriguing Super Bowl prop bets and novelty odds!


Understanding NFL Novelty Betting Odds

Just like standard sports bets, novelty odds are displayed in American moneyline format. Understanding these odds is crucial for making informed decisions:

  • A minus sign (-) indicates the favorite. For example, if "Heads" is -104, you would need to bet $104 to win $100 in profit.
  • A plus sign (+) indicates the underdog (or less likely outcome). If "Orange Gatorade" is +220, a $100 bet would yield $220 in profit if successful.

Remember that while some novelty bets like the coin toss are purely random, others like Gatorade color can be influenced by slight historical trends or team affiliations, though they remain largely speculative and fun-focused wagers.


Why Bet on NFL Novelty Betting Odds at FanDuel Sportsbook?


  • Unmatched Entertainment: Turn iconic, non-game moments into thrilling betting opportunities, amplifying your Super Bowl experience and boosting engagement beyond the final score.
  • Trusted and Secure Platform: Bet with confidence on a legally regulated and official betting partner, ensuring fair play, secure transactions, and data protection for all your wagers.
  • Real-Time Odds and Markets: Access up-to-the-minute NFL betting lines and competitive Super Bowl prop bets curated by expert oddsmakers, offering optimal value and dynamic adjustments.
  • Broad Appeal and Variety: Whether you're a seasoned bettor looking for unique NFL odds or a casual fan seeking a fun, low-stakes wager, novelty bets cater to a wide audience.
  • Responsible Gaming Focus: FanDuel provides robust tools and resources to help you bet responsibly, ensuring a safe and enjoyable betting environment while promoting healthy habits.

NFL Novelty Betting Odds Betting FAQs


  • What is an NFL novelty bet?

    An NFL novelty bet is a wager placed on a non-sporting outcome or an unusual event related to an NFL game, most commonly the Super Bowl. Unlike traditional bets on game results like moneylines or point spreads, novelty bets focus on unique aspects like the coin toss result, the length of the national anthem, or celebratory traditions such as the Gatorade shower.

  • Are NFL novelty bets purely based on luck?

    For some novelty bets, like the Super Bowl coin toss, the outcome is purely random with a 50/50 probability, making it entirely luck-based. For others, such as the Gatorade shower color, while still highly unpredictable, factors like historical frequency, team colors, or even superstitions can subtly influence the odds, though they remain primarily fun, speculative wagers rather than skill-based predictions.

  • What happens if there is no Gatorade shower for the winning coach?

    As explicitly stated in FanDuel's betting rules for this specific market, "All bets are void if there is no Gatorade shower for the winning head coach." This means your stake will be returned to you if the event does not occur, as the condition for the bet has not been met.

  • When do Super Bowl novelty odds become available on FanDuel?

    Odds for popular Super Bowl novelty prop bets, including the coin toss and Gatorade color, are typically released by FanDuel Sportsbook in the weeks leading up to the Super Bowl. It's advisable to check the platform regularly as the event approaches, as these markets can generate significant interest quickly.

  • Can I use promotions or bonuses for NFL novelty bets?

    FanDuel promotions and bonuses can often be applied to a wide range of betting markets, including NFL novelty bets. However, specific terms and conditions may apply to each promotion regarding eligibility, minimum odds, or bet types, so it's always best to review the details of any offer before placing your wager to ensure it qualifies.

NFL Player Props – Passing, Rushing, Touchdowns and More

FanDuel Sportsbook is your destination for NFL player prop betting—where every stat line, every matchup, and every breakout moment becomes a wagerable opportunity. Whether you're betting on a quarterback's passing yards, a running back’s TDs, or a receiver’s longest catch, FanDuel offers the most dynamic and data-driven prop markets in the industry.

With real-time odds, deep stat integrations, and a full slate of player performance bets for every NFL game, there’s no better place to lock in your prop picks all season long.


What Are NFL Player Props?

NFL player props are bets on individual player stats and achievements—perfect for fans who know their matchups and want to zero in on how specific athletes will perform. Popular NFL prop categories include:

  • Passing Yards
  • Passing Touchdowns
  • Rushing Yards
  • Rushing Touchdowns
  • Receiving Yards
  • Anytime Touchdown Scorer
  • Longest Reception or Rush
  • Completions/Attempts
  • Interceptions Thrown

FanDuel’s player props are powered by official NFL data and adjusted in real-time based on injuries, weather, betting action, and live updates.

Why Bettors Love NFL Player Props


  • Matchup-Based Strategy: Player props let you take advantage of individual matchups, trends, and injury news.
  • Data-Driven Value: Props are tied to measurable outcomes—perfect for bettors who track stats and projections.
  • Live-Betting Opportunities: Adjust your positions during the game with our live player prop odds.
  • Same Game Parlay (SGP) Friendly: Combine props into a single bet—for example: Lamar Jackson Over 225.5 passing yards + Zay Flowers Anytime TD + Ravens ML.

Why Choose FanDuel for NFL Player Props?


  • Official Sports Betting Partner of the NFL
  • Real-Time Updates: Live odds informed with official league data.

As one of the official betting partners of the National Football League, FanDuel gives you direct access to Super Bowl odds, league futures, and MVP betting markets — plus thousands of markets on all 32 teams.


How to Bet on NFL at FanDuel Sportsbook

At FanDuel, you can bet on NFL in ways that suit your strategy and level of experience. Here’s a clear beginner-to-intermediate guide on how to bet on the NFL, covering the core bet types and how to wager on the NFL at FanDuel Sportsbook.

Understanding the Main Types of NFL Bets

We offer a number of different betting markets for fans to pick from. Here are the most popular NFL bet types we offer:

  • NFL Spreads: The point spreads tries to level the playing field for both team's odds to win. A typical line might be: Chiefs -6.5 (+105) vs. Steelers +6.5 (-125). If you bet the Chiefs, they must win by 7+ points. If you back the Steelers, they can lose by 6 points—or win outright—and your bet cashes.

  • NFL Moneylines: These are the simplest form of NFL odds: pick the team you think will win outright. Favorites are denoted with a minus (-) and underdogs with a plus (+). For example: Lions -180 means you must wager $180 to win $100. Likewise, Bears +130 means a $100 bet returns $130 profit if they win.

  • NFL Totals (Over/Under): Bet on the total number of combined points in the game. If the Over/Under is set at 37.5: a 21-17 final score (38 combined points) hits the Over, while a 24-13 score (37 combined points) hits the Under.

  • Live NFL Odds (In-Game Betting): Watch and wager in real-time. Our in-play betting markets update between possessions and even between snaps. Bet on evolving outcomes like spreads, moneylines, totals, and player props as the game unfolds.

  • NFL Player Prop Bets: Target individual player stats such as passing yards for a QB, Anytime Touchdowns, rushing yards, total receptions, or interceptions. These markets are powered by official NFL data and constantly adjusted by FanDuel’s risk team to reflect real-world trends and injury status.

  • Parlays and Same Game Parlays: Combine multiple bets into a single wager for a bigger potential payout. Build your own Same Game Parlay for any NFL matchup by mixing player props, team totals, and game lines.

  • NFL Futures: Futures markets let you bet on long-term outcomes for the NFL regular season and playoffs. These include wagers such as the NFL Super Bowl winner, teams to win division titles, and which player will be crowned the NFL MVP.


Placing NFL Bets at FanDuel Sportsbook

With our website or mobile app, we make it easy to place bets on NFL events. Simply follow these steps:

  1. Sign up for a sportsbook account and veryify your ID and location if you haven't already

  2. Download the FanDuel Sportsbook app or login at sportsbook.fanduel.com on a desktop device

  3. Deposit funds

  4. Browse the NFL odds and props currently available. Click the odds to add them to your bet slip

  5. Enter a wager amount and add any promos or bonus bets you have available. The betslip will automatically calculate your potential payout. Click to place the bet when you are happy with your betslip

  6. Track your progress in the My Bets section


How to Read NFL Odds

NFL odds at FanDuel Sportsbook use the American odds format, shown as either positive (+) or negative (−) numbers:

  • +200: Wager $100 to win $200 (underdog).
  • -150: Wager $150 to win $100 (favorite).

Example: If you bet $100 on the Detroit Lions +200, you’d receive $300 total if they win: $200 in profit plus your $100 stake. Betting Philadelphia Eagles at −150 requires risking $150 to profit $100. FanDuel’s bet slip automatically calculates your payout, helping you make informed, fast decisions.


Why FanDuel is Trusted for NFL Betting


  • Trusted Market Leader: Official betting partner of the NFL.
  • Real-Time Odds: Adjusted by in-house analysts and informed by live market activity.
  • Flexible Markets: From player props to live Same Game Parlays and Super Bowl futures.
  • Responsible Gaming Tools: Deposit limits, timeouts, and support always available.

NFL Odds and Betting FAQs


  • Do overtime periods count for my NFL spread, moneyline, and totals wagers?

    Yes. FanDuel grades almost every NFL game market—including full-game spreads, moneylines, and over/unders—on the final official score after all overtime periods are completed, unless the market header specifically says otherwise.

  • What happens if an NFL game ends in a tie?

    If the final score is tied after overtime, moneyline wagers are void and stakes are refunded, while point-spread and totals bets are settled on the posted numbers (so a spread that lands exactly on the closing line is graded as a push).

  • What happens if an NFL game is postponed or moved to a different stadium?

    When a game is postponed before kickoff, all wagers are void. If it’s relocated but still played on the scheduled date—whether to a neutral site or the opponent’s stadium—bets remain live because FanDuel grades action on the official league box score, not the venue.

  • How does FanDuel treat a Same Game Parlay if one NFL leg is void (for example, because the game ended in a tie)?

    If a leg is voided, the parlay is re-priced to the odds of the remaining legs and still stands. Only when every leg in the SGP is void does FanDuel return the entire stake.
